config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society’s interests matter a lot, but they should not automatically come before individual rights, because that can easily become an excuse to abuse power. Rights like free expression, fair treatment, and personal dignity are the guardrails that keep society from becoming oppressive. From my perspective, the better principle is to balance both, with individual rights protected unless there is a very strong and clearly justified public need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami sangat dipengaruhi oleh kecurigaan terhadap kekuasaan yang terlalu besar, jadi hak individu bukan sekadar aksesori; itu justru batas penting agar negara atau mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi kalau dijadikan alasan utama tanpa batas yang jelas, itu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk membungkam kebebasan, mengorbankan minoritas, atau mengabaikan martabat pribadi. Bagi saya, yang paling masuk akal adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya, dengan hak individu sebagai dasar yang hanya bisa dibatasi bila ada kebutuhan publik yang sangat kuat dan benar-benar dapat dibuktikan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests above individual rights as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand why people emphasize harmony, order, and collective welfare, but I have also seen how easily “the public good” can be used to silence criticism or excuse abuse of power. Rights should be the baseline, not a luxury, because without them the majority or the state can trample minorities and ordinary people. Society matters, but it should be built through respect for individual dignity, not by treating the individual as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, justru pengalaman sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa mayoritas dan negara sama-sama bisa menyalahgunakan kekuasaan kalau hak individu tidak dijadikan batas yang tegas. Saya juga memahami argumen tentang harmoni dan kesejahteraan bersama, tetapi bagi saya itu terlalu mudah dipakai sebagai alasan untuk membungkam kritik atau mengorbankan minoritas. Kepentingan sosial itu penting, namun harus dicapai tanpa memperlakukan hak individu sebagai sesuatu yang bisa dikesampingkan kapan saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, I understand the appeal of harmony, order, and collective responsibility, but I have also seen how easily “for the common good” can be used to silence criticism or excuse abuse of power. Individual rights have to be the baseline, because without them the state or the majority can trample minorities and ordinary people. Society is stronger when it protects dignity and freedom first, not when it treats the individual as something disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan yang berlebihan, menekan minoritas, atau membungkam kritik, dan itu justru berbahaya bagi masyarakat sendiri. Kepentingan publik memang nyata, tetapi ia tidak boleh menjadi cek kosong untuk mengorbankan kebebasan berbicara, proses yang adil, dan martabat pribadi. Bagi saya, hak individu harus jadi batas dasar, lalu pembatasannya hanya sah jika ada kebutuhan publik yang sangat kuat, sempit, dan benar-benar bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
